Understanding landscape factors, including suites geographic environmental variables, both historical contemporary ecological evolutionary processes shape distribution genetic diversity primary goal landscape conservation genetics particularly consequential species involved ecological restoration. this study, examine factors that shape distribution genetic variation leguminous shrub (Caragana microphylla) important restoration efforts Mongolian Plateau China. This region houses several major bioclimatic gradients, microphylla important restoration species because stabilizes soils prevents advancing desertification Inner Mongolia Plateau caused ongoing climate change.
assembled expansive genomic dataset, consisting microsatellite loci, four cpDNA regions, 5788 genome-wide SNPs from populations microphylla. then applied ecological niche modelling linear non-linear regression techniques investigate historical contemporary forces that explain patterns genetic diversity population structure microphylla Inner Mongolia Plateau. found strong evidence that both geographic environmental heterogeneity contribute genetic differentiation that spatial distribution genetic diversity microphylla appears result partly from presence glacial refugium southwestern edge current range.
These results suggest that geographic, environmental, historical factors have contributed spatial genetic variation this ecologically important species. These results should guide restoration plans sustain genetic diversity during plant translocations.

such species Caragana microphylla, perennial sandy grassland desert deciduous shrub species belonging legume family (Fabaceae). Native temperate Asia, including Siberia, Mongolia, China microphylla widely distributed shrub species northern steppe agro-pastoral ecotone China. high plain Inner Mongolia Plateau, microphylla component shrub steppe landscape, sandy land steppe dominant species vegetation species been valued tolerance heat, cold, drought resistance wind erosion, sand burial, hail storms. been used pioneer leguminous shrub species vegetation rehabilitation stabilization widely degraded degrading grasslands China, because ability serve windbreak capacity carbon fixation, nitrogen fixation, nutrient accumulation sandy soils also served supplemental livestock forage with high nutrient value Genetic variation population structure wild microphylla from Inner Mongolia Plateau have been evaluated different marker systems, including AFLPs, RAPDs, microsatellites [13-17], previous studies have quantified contributions spatial genetic divergence this system. Inner Mongolia Plateau characterized pronounced biophysical gradients, presenting opportunity investigate effects multiple geographic environmental factors population connectivity. temperature gradient runs roughly North-South, while precipitation gradient runs from arid regions Southwest wetter regions Northeast. ecotone largely tracks precipitation gradient, transitioning from desert Southwest grassland (high meadow steppe) central plateau forest Northeast, with pockets shrublands sandy lands Southeast. Here, evaluated population genetic divergence microphylla across entire geographic range Inner Mongolia Plateau, using polymorphic microsatellite markers, four cpDNA sequences 5788 SNPs generated through (GBS). major goals this study were characterize genetic variation population structure microphylla, (ii) quantify relative contributions geographic factors (IBD) environmental clines (IBE) genetic differentiation this important restoration species.

Thus, overall, study presents strong case role both historic contemporary factors, including both geographic environmental variables, generating currently observed patterns spatial genetic variation microphylla. plants involved ecological restoration, like microphylla, understanding these patterns critical, because restoration work inherently involves moving plants between areas (even they geographically close). instance, this system, populations exhibit genetic differentiation between environments with different temperature regimes, therefore restoration efforts should focus transplanting between areas with similar environmental conditions. Plants adapted different thermal climates flower wrong time sync with local population, reducing effectiveness transplant efforts. Because microphylla also show geographically driven genetic differentiation, plants that transplanted from distant sites that environmentally similar could effective introducing genetic variation into struggling populations Hence, better understanding factors that shape genetic variation these species critical preventing unintended consequences reintroductions translocations guiding conservation plans produce best possible outcomes. Specifically, knowledge generated studies like ours contribute managing genetic variation distributed these species, probability individuals surviving becoming established under various climatic factors, chances maintaining genetically healthy populations.
